zoning_laws: In Los Angeles, CA, multi-family zoning regulations include the following key metrics: 1. **Minimum Lot Area**: Every lot must have a minimum width of 50 feet and a minimum area of 5,000 square feet. 2. **Height Limits**: Residential buildings typically cannot exceed 30 feet. 3. **Passageway Requirement**: A 10-foot passageway is required from the street to one entrance of each dwelling unit, except for specific zones. Specific details can be confirmed in the Los Angeles Municipal Code (LAMC) and from the Department of City Planning.
